questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This question assesses the ability to design a secure, compliant NL-to-SQL system using Azure OpenAI while implementing enterprise-grade governance, auditing, and multi-tenant isolation. The focus is on integration patterns between key Azure services (API Management, Blob Storage, Cognitive Search) and practical safety mechanisms.

## Key Concepts
- Natural language to SQL translation with robust guardrails
- Read-only database access with schema whitelisting
- PII detection and redaction in user prompts
- Multi-tenant data isolation and security
- End-to-end data flow architecture with comprehensive auditing

## Code Example
```python
# Schema guardrails implementation
ALLOWED_SCHEMAS = {'sales', 'marketing', 'analytics'}
import re

def is_safe_sql(sql_query):
    # Check for read-only operations
    forbidden_patterns = r'\b(insert|update|delete|drop|create|alter)\b'
    if re.search(forbidden_patterns, sql_query, re.IGNORECASE):
        return False
    
    # Verify schema access
    schema_pattern = r'\b(from|join)\s+([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)'
    schemas = re.findall(schema_pattern, sql_query, re.IGNORECASE)
    return all(schema.lower() in ALLOWED_SCHEMAS for _, schema in schemas)
```

```javascript
function redactPII(text) {
  return text
    .replace(/\b[A-Z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}\b/gi, "[REDACTED_EMAIL]")
    .replace(/\b\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b/g, "[REDACTED_PHONE]");
}
```

```javascript
function postFilter(text) {

  return blocked.test(text) ? "[REDACTED_CONTENT]" : text;
}
```

## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design cross-model orchestration with data residency and cost controls in a multi-tenant Azure context, plus security and observability considerations.

## Key Concepts

- Multi-model routing between Azure OpenAI and private LLMs
- Per-tenant policy store in Cosmos DB (model choice, residency, latency, cost)
- API Management as the gateway with secure, auditable routing
- Decision engine weighing latency and cost budgets
- PII redaction and Data Lake audit logging

## Code Example

```javascript
async function selectEndpoint(tenantId, latencyMs, cost) {
  const policy = await getPolicy(tenantId); // from Cosmos DB
  if (latencyMs > policy.latencyBudgetMs || cost > policy.costBudget) {
    return 'private-llm';
  }
  return policy.preferredModel === 'openai' ? 'azure-openai' : 'private-llm';
}
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you validate the decision engine with synthetic tenants and varying latency?
- What strategies ensure policy updates are atomic and live without disrupting in-flight requests?
